rulings.Calendar Matter: Esther Eames Notice Of Motion And Motion To Remove And Replace Special Master, Michael Pecherer For Unreasonable Delay
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2023 line 1. ESTHER EAMES' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO REMOVE AND REPLACE SPECIAL MASTER, MICHAEL PECHERER, FOR UNREASONABLE DELAY Status Report. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN SHOCKLEY ET AL VS. PANTOLL HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Moving party failed to show that different theories of liability asserted in Plaintiffs' various causes of action are duplicative warranting their elimination from the operative complaint. The Court notes that, assuming that Defendants are correct that these causes of action are entirely duplicative and add not legal or factual disputes to this action, their elimination will not serve any purpose, e.g. narrowing the issues for discovery or trial. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN SHOCKLEY ET AL VS. PANTOLL HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Cody Chapman, Hans Geiszler, And Sutter Street Sf, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is denied as to the request to strike punitive damages. Plaintiffs sufficiently alleges facts to support conscious disregard for Plaintiff's rights. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the word "Defendant" in paragraph 9 to be replaced with "Defendants' predecessor-in-interest" as requested in opposition. Plaintiff shall have a 10 day leave to file an Amendment to Complaint limited to this issue. Motion is denied without leave to amend as to the allegations in paragraph 25 pertaining to the events occurring on or about June 22, 2020 since Plaintiff alleges to have vacated the premises on April 5, 2020. Motion is denied as to paragraphs 13, 16, 19, 21, 24, 27, 30, 34, 37. Moving party failed to show that these paraphs are false, irrelevant or improper. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN M SINCLAIR VS. KEN KESLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF JOHN SINCLAIR'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is GRANTED as follows. The "Counterclaim" (starting at page 11 line 2) against Plaintiff and the prayer for punitive damage (at page 13, line 16) are stricken from the Amended Answer. Amended Answer shall otherwise remain the operative answer. Defendant shall have 10-days from notice of entry of order to re-file his "counterclaim" as a separate Cross-Complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that it is entitled to attorney's fees under the circumstances of this case. The significance of the attorney's fees clause provision is not discussed in the moving papers and is not supported by any authority ("If the party which shall have commenced or instituted the action, suit or proceeding shall dismiss or discontinue it without the concurrence of the other party, such other party shall be deemed the prevailing party."); CC 1717(b) is not addressed in the moving papers; Hartbrodt v. Burke (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 168 and Gray v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 165, 173, while cited are not analyzed. Specifically, it is not addressed how factually or procedurally those cases are controlling in the instant situation where the voluntary dismissal has not been set aside and there has never been a ruling on the merits of the demurrer. The demurrer was taken off calendar after an uncontested tentative ruling became the order of the Court on January 27, 2023. While the demurrer might have been meritorious, the Court never issues a ruling thereon, dismissal having been filed long before the opposition was due. Defendant never asked the Court to disregard the voluntary dismissal and rule on the demurrer. Unauthorized sur-reply is stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
